cards.title: Safe, Respectful, Responsible Culture Promise
cards.teaches: This session teaches safe, respectful, responsible culture promise as an explicit part of the Matchbook culture operating system.
cards.why: It matters because students cannot be expected to live culture spine without being taught the specific behaviors, language, and routines that make the system visible.
cards.srr: Defines the visible behaviors for Safe, Respectful, and Responsible so values become observable and teachable.
cards.adult: Model the routine, name the expectation, practice the steps, recognize visible evidence, and reteach the smallest broken part before moving to consequence language.
cards.student: Students name what the routine requires, practice the steps, respond to the cue, and show evidence of Safe, Respectful, and Responsible behavior.

grade.K-2: Use concrete language, visuals, gestures, and repeated practice.
grade.3-5: Use examples, non-examples, peer practice, and student explanation.
grade.6-8: Use rationale, dignity, autonomy, and student leadership in execution.

cards.resourceNeeded: Teacher guide, student mini-lesson, slide sequence, rehearsal card, coaching look-for, and grade-band translation as applicable.
cards.rehearsal: Model it once, practice it once, stop at the first predictable slip, rebuild that exact move, then run it again cleanly.
cards.recognition: Recognize named behavior tied to Safe, Respectful, or Responsible. Do not reward vague compliance.
cards.reteach: Reteach the routine before assuming defiance. Keep the reteach narrow, visible, and immediate.
cards.coach: Look for adult clarity, student independence, low adult narration, and evidence that the routine can survive a predictable distraction.

r3.Reflection: What do I notice about how this routine is working in real time?
r3.Resistance: What might get in the way for adults or students during implementation?
r3.Revelation: What next move will help the routine become more consistent tomorrow?

cards.why: It matters because students cannot be expected to live daily anchor routines without being taught the specific behaviors, language, and routines that make the system visible.
cards.srr: Builds calm, relational, responsible entry into learning while protecting student dignity and readiness.
cards.adult: Model the routine, name the expectation, practice the steps, recognize visible evidence, and reteach the smallest broken part before moving to consequence language.
cards.student: Students name what the routine requires, practice the steps, respond to the cue, and show evidence of Safe, Respectful, and Responsible behavior.

grade.K-2: Use visuals, adult-coached words, short reset choices, and immediate return practice.
grade.3-5: Use sentence stems, role-play, peer repair language, and guided reflection.
grade.6-8: Use dignity-centered language, private correction, student-owned repair, and independent return expectations.

cards.resourceNeeded: Teacher guide, student mini-lesson, slide sequence, rehearsal card, coaching look-for, and grade-band translation as applicable.
cards.rehearsal: Model it once, practice it once, stop at the first predictable slip, rebuild that exact move, then run it again cleanly.
cards.recognition: Recognize named behavior tied to Safe, Respectful, or Responsible. Do not reward vague compliance.
cards.reteach: Reteach the routine before assuming defiance. Keep the reteach narrow, visible, and immediate.
cards.coach: Look for adult clarity, student independence, low adult narration, and evidence that the routine can survive a predictable distraction.

r3.Reflection: What do I notice about how this routine is working in real time?
r3.Resistance: What might get in the way for adults or students during implementation?
r3.Revelation: What next move will help the routine become more consistent tomorrow?
